prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes that stock prices follow a log-normal distribution. Historical daily adjusted close prices for PYPL over the past year were analyzed to estimate the daily drift (mean log return) and daily volatility (standard deviation of log returns). Specifically, an annualized drift of approximately -15% and an annualized volatility of 40% were used, converted to daily terms. These parameters were then used to project the price for 18 trading days, incorporating the current price of $43.92. The point estimate represents the median expected price, and the confidence intervals are derived from the log-normal distribution using standard Z-scores for the respective confidence levels (0.6745 for 50%, 1.2816 for 80%, 1.6449 for 90%).
